reasoning: The FrontierMath benchmark was introduced in late 2024, featuring extremely difficult, novel problems that demand hours or days of expert mathematician effort. At launch, leading models (including Claude 3.5 Sonnet, GPT-4o, and Gemini 1.5 Pro) scored below 2% even with code execution and extended thinking time. Historically, improving from <5% to >80% on major benchmarks like MATH or MMLU has taken roughly 3 years of concerted scaling and algorithmic advances. While major labs are pursuing advanced test-time compute and reinforcement learning protocols (e.g., OpenAI o1, Google AlphaProof), scaling performance from 2% to over 80% on graduate-level, historically unprecedented problems within a single year (by the end of 2025) would completely break historical trends and require a near-superhuman leap in long-horizon mathematical reasoning. Given this baseline trajectory, it is highly unlikely to reach 80% before the end of 2025.
